# types

```go
import "go/types"
```

Package types declares the data types and implements the algorithms for type\-checking of Go packages. Use [Config.Check](<#Config.Check>) to invoke the type checker for a package. Alternatively, create a new type checker with [NewChecker](<#NewChecker>) and invoke it incrementally by calling [Checker.Files](<#Checker.Files>).

Type\-checking consists of several interdependent phases:

Name resolution maps each identifier \(\[ast.Ident\]\) in the program to the symbol \([Object](<#Object>)\) it denotes. Use the Defs and Uses fields of [Info](<#Info>) or the [Info.ObjectOf](<#Info.ObjectOf>) method to find the symbol for an identifier, and use the Implicits field of [Info](<#Info>) to find the symbol for certain other kinds of syntax node.

Constant folding computes the exact constant value \(\[constant.Value\]\) of every expression \(\[ast.Expr\]\) that is a compile\-time constant. Use the Types field of [Info](<#Info>) to find the results of constant folding for an expression.

Type deduction computes the type \([Type](<#Type>)\) of every expression \(\[ast.Expr\]\) and checks for compliance with the language specification. Use the Types field of [Info](<#Info>) for the results of type deduction.

Applications that need to type\-check one or more complete packages of Go source code may find it more convenient not to invoke the type checker directly but instead to use the Load function in package [golang.org/x/tools/go/packages](<https://pkg.go.dev/golang.org/x/tools/go/packages/>).

For a tutorial, see https://go.dev/s/types-tutorial.

## Constants

<a name="PackageVar"></a>

```go
const (
    PackageVar // a package-level variable
    LocalVar   // a local variable
    RecvVar    // a method receiver variable
    ParamVar   // a function parameter variable
    ResultVar  // a function result variable
    FieldVar   // a struct field
)
```

## Variables

<a name="Typ"></a>Typ contains the predeclared \*Basic types indexed by their corresponding BasicKind.

The \*Basic type for Typ\[Byte\] will have the name "uint8". Use Universe.Lookup\("byte"\).Type\(\) to obtain the specific alias basic type named "byte" \(and analogous for "rune"\).

```go
var Typ = []*Basic{
    Invalid: {Invalid, 0, "invalid type"},

    Bool:          {Bool, IsBoolean, "bool"},
    Int:           {Int, IsInteger, "int"},
    Int8:          {Int8, IsInteger, "int8"},
    Int16:         {Int16, IsInteger, "int16"},
    Int32:         {Int32, IsInteger, "int32"},
    Int64:         {Int64, IsInteger, "int64"},
    Uint:          {Uint, IsInteger | IsUnsigned, "uint"},
    Uint8:         {Uint8, IsInteger | IsUnsigned, "uint8"},
    Uint16:        {Uint16, IsInteger | IsUnsigned, "uint16"},
    Uint32:        {Uint32, IsInteger | IsUnsigned, "uint32"},
    Uint64:        {Uint64, IsInteger | IsUnsigned, "uint64"},
    Uintptr:       {Uintptr, IsInteger | IsUnsigned, "uintptr"},
    Float32:       {Float32, IsFloat, "float32"},
    Float64:       {Float64, IsFloat, "float64"},
    Complex64:     {Complex64, IsComplex, "complex64"},
    Complex128:    {Complex128, IsComplex, "complex128"},
    String:        {String, IsString, "string"},
    UnsafePointer: {UnsafePointer, 0, "Pointer"},

    UntypedBool:    {UntypedBool, IsBoolean | IsUntyped, "untyped bool"},
    UntypedInt:     {UntypedInt, IsInteger | IsUntyped, "untyped int"},
    UntypedRune:    {UntypedRune, IsInteger | IsUntyped, "untyped rune"},
    UntypedFloat:   {UntypedFloat, IsFloat | IsUntyped, "untyped float"},
    UntypedComplex: {UntypedComplex, IsComplex | IsUntyped, "untyped complex"},
    UntypedString:  {UntypedString, IsString | IsUntyped, "untyped string"},
    UntypedNil:     {UntypedNil, IsUntyped, "untyped nil"},
}
```

<a name="AssertableTo"></a>

## func [AssertableTo](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api_predicates.go#L19>)

```go
func AssertableTo(V *Interface, T Type) bool
```

AssertableTo reports whether a value of type V can be asserted to have type T.

The behavior of AssertableTo is unspecified in three cases:

- if T is Typ\[Invalid\]
- if V is a generalized interface; i.e., an interface that may only be used as a type constraint in Go code
- if T is an uninstantiated generic type

<a name="AssignableTo"></a>

## func [AssignableTo](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api_predicates.go#L33>)

```go
func AssignableTo(V, T Type) bool
```

AssignableTo reports whether a value of type V is assignable to a variable of type T.

The behavior of AssignableTo is unspecified if V or T is Typ\[Invalid\] or an uninstantiated generic type.

<a name="CheckExpr"></a>

## func [CheckExpr](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/eval.go#L56>)

```go
func CheckExpr(fset *token.FileSet, pkg *Package, pos token.Pos, expr ast.Expr, info *Info) (err error)
```

CheckExpr type checks the expression expr as if it had appeared at position pos of package pkg. [Type](<#Type>) information about the expression is recorded in info. The expression may be an identifier denoting an uninstantiated generic function or type.

If pkg == nil, the [Universe](<#Universe>) scope is used and the provided position pos is ignored. If pkg \!= nil, and pos is invalid, the package scope is used. Otherwise, pos must belong to the package.

An error is returned if pos is not within the package or if the node cannot be type\-checked.

Note: [Eval](<#Eval>) and CheckExpr should not be used instead of running Check to compute types and values, but in addition to Check, as these functions ignore the context in which an expression is used \(e.g., an assignment\). Thus, top\-level untyped constants will return an untyped type rather than the respective context\-specific type.

<a name="Comparable"></a>

## func [Comparable](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/predicates.go#L153>)

```go
func Comparable(T Type) bool
```

Comparable reports whether values of type T are comparable.

<a name="ConvertibleTo"></a>

## func [ConvertibleTo](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api_predicates.go#L44>)

```go
func ConvertibleTo(V, T Type) bool
```

ConvertibleTo reports whether a value of type V is convertible to a value of type T.

The behavior of ConvertibleTo is unspecified if V or T is Typ\[Invalid\] or an uninstantiated generic type.

<a name="DefPredeclaredTestFuncs"></a>

```go
func DefPredeclaredTestFuncs()
```

DefPredeclaredTestFuncs defines the assert and trace built\-ins. These built\-ins are intended for debugging and testing of this package only.

<a name="ExprString"></a>

## func [ExprString](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/exprstring.go#L18>)

```go
func ExprString(x ast.Expr) string
```

ExprString returns the \(possibly shortened\) string representation for x. Shortened representations are suitable for user interfaces but may not necessarily follow Go syntax.

<a name="Id"></a>

## func [Id](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L76>)

```go
func Id(pkg *Package, name string) string
```

Id returns name if it is exported, otherwise it returns the name qualified with the package path.

<a name="Identical"></a>

## func [Identical](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api_predicates.go#L88>)

```go
func Identical(x, y Type) bool
```

Identical reports whether x and y are identical types. Receivers of [Signature](<#Signature>) types are ignored.

Predicates such as [Identical](<#Identical>), [Implements](<#Implements>), and [Satisfies](<#Satisfies>) assume that both operands belong to a consistent collection of symbols \([Object](<#Object>) values\). For example, two [Named](<#Named>) types can be identical only if their [Named.Obj](<#Named.Obj>) methods return the same [TypeName](<#TypeName>) symbol. A collection of symbols is consistent if, for each logical package whose path is P, the creation of those symbols involved at most one call to [NewPackage](<#NewPackage>)\(P, ...\). To ensure consistency, use a single [Importer](<#Importer>) for all loaded packages and their dependencies. For more information, see https://github.com/golang/go/issues/57497.

<a name="IdenticalIgnoreTags"></a>

## func [IdenticalIgnoreTags](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api_predicates.go#L95>)

```go
func IdenticalIgnoreTags(x, y Type) bool
```

IdenticalIgnoreTags reports whether x and y are identical types if tags are ignored. Receivers of [Signature](<#Signature>) types are ignored.

<a name="Implements"></a>

## func [Implements](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api_predicates.go#L53>)

```go
func Implements(V Type, T *Interface) bool
```

Implements reports whether type V implements interface T.

The behavior of Implements is unspecified if V is Typ\[Invalid\] or an uninstantiated generic type.

<a name="Satisfies"></a>

<a name="SelectionString"></a>

## func [SelectionString](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/selection.go#L159>)

```go
func SelectionString(s *Selection, qf Qualifier) string
```

SelectionString returns the string form of s. The Qualifier controls the printing of package\-level objects, and may be nil.

Examples:

```go
"field (T) f int"
"method (T) f(X) Y"
"method expr (T) f(X) Y"
```

<a name="Alias"></a>

## type [Alias](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/alias.go#L45-L52>)

An Alias represents an alias type.

Alias types are created by alias declarations such as:

```go
type A = int
```

The type on the right\-hand side of the declaration can be accessed using [Alias.Rhs](<#Alias.Rhs>). This type may itself be an alias. Call [Unalias](<#Unalias>) to obtain the first non\-alias type in a chain of alias type declarations.

Like a defined \([Named](<#Named>)\) type, an alias type has a name. Use the [Alias.Obj](<#Alias.Obj>) method to access its [TypeName](<#TypeName>) object.

Historically, Alias types were not materialized so that, in the example above, A's type was represented by a Basic \(int\), not an Alias whose [Alias.Rhs](<#Alias.Rhs>) is int. But Go 1.24 allows you to declare an alias type with type parameters or arguments:

```go
type Set[K comparable] = map[K]bool
s := make(Set[String])
```

and this requires that Alias types be materialized. Use the [Alias.TypeParams](<#Alias.TypeParams>) and [Alias.TypeArgs](<#Alias.TypeArgs>) methods to access them.

To ease the transition, the Alias type was introduced in go1.22, but the type\-checker would not construct values of this type unless the GODEBUG=gotypesalias=1 environment variable was provided. Starting in go1.23, this variable is enabled by default. This setting also causes the predeclared type "any" to be represented as an Alias, not a bare [Interface](<#Interface>).

```go
type Alias struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewAlias"></a>

### func [NewAlias](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/alias.go#L56>)

```go
func NewAlias(obj *TypeName, rhs Type) *Alias
```

NewAlias creates a new Alias type with the given type name and rhs. If rhs is nil, the alias is incomplete.

<a name="Alias.Obj"></a>

### func \(\*Alias\) [Obj](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/alias.go#L65>)

```go
func (a *Alias) Obj() *TypeName
```

Obj returns the type name for the declaration defining the alias type a. For instantiated types, this is same as the type name of the origin type.

<a name="Alias.Origin"></a>

### func \(\*Alias\) [Origin](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/alias.go#L78>)

```go
func (a *Alias) Origin() *Alias
```

Origin returns the generic Alias type of which a is an instance. If a is not an instance of a generic alias, Origin returns a.

<a name="Alias.Rhs"></a>

### func \(\*Alias\) [Rhs](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/alias.go#L97>)

```go
func (a *Alias) Rhs() Type
```

Rhs returns the type R on the right\-hand side of an alias declaration "type A = R", which may be another alias.

<a name="Alias.SetTypeParams"></a>

### func \(\*Alias\) [SetTypeParams](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/alias.go#L86>)

```go
func (a *Alias) SetTypeParams(tparams []*TypeParam)
```

SetTypeParams sets the type parameters of the alias type a. The alias a must not have type arguments.

<a name="Alias.String"></a>

### func \(\*Alias\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/alias.go#L67>)

```go
func (a *Alias) String() string
```

<a name="Alias.TypeArgs"></a>

### func \(\*Alias\) [TypeArgs](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/alias.go#L93>)

```go
func (a *Alias) TypeArgs() *TypeList
```

TypeArgs returns the type arguments used to instantiate the Alias type. If a is not an instance of a generic alias, the result is nil.

<a name="Alias.TypeParams"></a>

### func \(\*Alias\) [TypeParams](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/alias.go#L82>)

```go
func (a *Alias) TypeParams() *TypeParamList
```

TypeParams returns the type parameters of the alias type a, or nil. A generic Alias and its instances have the same type parameters.

<a name="Alias.Underlying"></a>

### func \(\*Alias\) [Underlying](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/alias.go#L74>)

```go
func (a *Alias) Underlying() Type
```

Underlying returns the [underlying type](<https://go.dev/ref/spec#Underlying_types.>) of the alias type a, which is the underlying type of the aliased type. Underlying types are never Named, TypeParam, or Alias types.

<a name="BasicInfo"></a>

## type [BasicInfo](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/basic.go#L51>)

BasicInfo is a set of flags describing properties of a basic type.

```go
type BasicInfo int
```

<a name="IsBoolean"></a>Properties of basic types.

```go
const (
    IsBoolean BasicInfo = 1 << iota
    IsInteger
    IsUnsigned
    IsFloat
    IsComplex
    IsString
    IsUntyped

    IsOrdered   = IsInteger | IsFloat | IsString
    IsNumeric   = IsInteger | IsFloat | IsComplex
    IsConstType = IsBoolean | IsNumeric | IsString
)
```

<a name="BasicKind"></a>

## type [BasicKind](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/basic.go#L11>)

BasicKind describes the kind of basic type.

```go
type BasicKind int
```

<a name="Invalid"></a>

```go
const (
    Invalid BasicKind = iota // type is invalid

    // predeclared types
    Bool
    Int
    Int8
    Int16
    Int32
    Int64
    Uint
    Uint8
    Uint16
    Uint32
    Uint64
    Uintptr
    Float32
    Float64
    Complex64
    Complex128
    String
    UnsafePointer

    // types for untyped values
    UntypedBool
    UntypedInt
    UntypedRune
    UntypedFloat
    UntypedComplex
    UntypedString
    UntypedNil

    // aliases
    Byte = Uint8
    Rune = Int32
)
```

<a name="Checker"></a>

## type [Checker](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/check.go#L156-L209>)

A Checker maintains the state of the type checker. It must be created with [NewChecker](<#NewChecker>).

```go
type Checker struct {
    *Info
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewChecker"></a>

### func [NewChecker](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/check.go#L302>)

```go
func NewChecker(conf *Config, fset *token.FileSet, pkg *Package, info *Info) *Checker
```

NewChecker returns a new [Checker](<#Checker>) instance for a given package. [Package](<#Package>) files may be added incrementally via checker.Files.

<a name="Checker.Files"></a>

### func \(\*Checker\) [Files](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/check.go#L478>)

```go
func (check *Checker) Files(files []*ast.File) (err error)
```

Files checks the provided files as part of the checker's package.

<a name="Config"></a>

## type [Config](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L122-L199>)

A Config specifies the configuration for type checking. The zero value for Config is a ready\-to\-use default configuration.

```go
type Config struct {
    // Context is the context used for resolving global identifiers. If nil, the
    // type checker will initialize this field with a newly created context.
    Context *Context

    // GoVersion describes the accepted Go language version. The string must
    // start with a prefix of the form "go%d.%d" (e.g. "go1.20", "go1.21rc1", or
    // "go1.21.0") or it must be empty; an empty string disables Go language
    // version checks. If the format is invalid, invoking the type checker will
    // result in an error.
    GoVersion string

    // If IgnoreFuncBodies is set, function bodies are not
    // type-checked.
    IgnoreFuncBodies bool

    // If FakeImportC is set, `import "C"` (for packages requiring Cgo)
    // declares an empty "C" package and errors are omitted for qualified
    // identifiers referring to package C (which won't find an object).
    // This feature is intended for the standard library cmd/api tool.
    //
    // Caution: Effects may be unpredictable due to follow-on errors.
    //          Do not use casually!
    FakeImportC bool

    // If Error != nil, it is called with each error found
    // during type checking; err has dynamic type Error.
    // Secondary errors (for instance, to enumerate all types
    // involved in an invalid recursive type declaration) have
    // error strings that start with a '\t' character.
    // If Error == nil, type-checking stops with the first
    // error found.
    Error func(err error)

    // An importer is used to import packages referred to from
    // import declarations.
    // If the installed importer implements ImporterFrom, the type
    // checker calls ImportFrom instead of Import.
    // The type checker reports an error if an importer is needed
    // but none was installed.
    Importer Importer

    // If Sizes != nil, it provides the sizing functions for package unsafe.
    // Otherwise SizesFor("gc", "amd64") is used instead.
    Sizes Sizes

    // If DisableUnusedImportCheck is set, packages are not checked
    // for unused imports.
    DisableUnusedImportCheck bool
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Config.Check"></a>

### func \(\*Config\) [Check](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L490>)

```go
func (conf *Config) Check(path string, fset *token.FileSet, files []*ast.File, info *Info) (*Package, error)
```

Check type\-checks a package and returns the resulting package object and the first error if any. Additionally, if info \!= nil, Check populates each of the non\-nil maps in the [Info](<#Info>) struct.

The package is marked as complete if no errors occurred, otherwise it is incomplete. See [Config.Error](<#Config>) for controlling behavior in the presence of errors.

The package is specified by a list of \*ast.Files and corresponding file set, and the package path the package is identified with. The clean path must not be empty or dot \("."\).

<a name="Const"></a>

## type [Const](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L221-L224>)

A Const represents a declared constant.

```go
type Const struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewConst"></a>

### func [NewConst](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L228>)

```go
func NewConst(pos token.Pos, pkg *Package, name string, typ Type, val constant.Value) *Const
```

NewConst returns a new constant with value val. The remaining arguments set the attributes found with all Objects.

<a name="Const.String"></a>

### func \(\*Const\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L647>)

```go
func (obj *Const) String() string
```

<a name="Const.Val"></a>

### func \(\*Const\) [Val](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L233>)

```go
func (obj *Const) Val() constant.Value
```

Val returns the constant's value.

<a name="Context"></a>

## type [Context](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/context.go#L44-L49>)

A Context is an opaque type checking context. It may be used to share identical type instances across type\-checked packages or calls to Instantiate. Contexts are safe for concurrent use.

The use of a shared context does not guarantee that identical instances are deduplicated in all cases.

```go
type Context struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewContext"></a>

### func [NewContext](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/context.go#L58>)

```go
func NewContext() *Context
```

NewContext creates a new Context.

<a name="Error"></a>

## type [Error](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L51-L65>)

An Error describes a type\-checking error; it implements the error interface. A "soft" error is an error that still permits a valid interpretation of a package \(such as "unused variable"\); "hard" errors may lead to unpredictable behavior if ignored.

```go
type Error struct {
    Fset *token.FileSet // file set for interpretation of Pos
    Pos  token.Pos      // error position
    Msg  string         // error message
    Soft bool           // if set, error is "soft"
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Error.Error"></a>

### func \(Error\) [Error](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L69>)

```go
func (err Error) Error() string
```

Error returns an error string formatted as follows: filename:line:column: message

<a name="Func"></a>

## type [Func](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L400-L404>)

A Func represents a declared function, concrete method, or abstract \(interface\) method. Its Type\(\) is always a \*Signature. An abstract method may belong to many interfaces due to embedding.

```go
type Func struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="MissingMethod"></a>

### func [MissingMethod](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/lookup.go#L365>)

```go
func MissingMethod(V Type, T *Interface, static bool) (method *Func, wrongType bool)
```

MissingMethod returns \(nil, false\) if V implements T, otherwise it returns a missing method required by T and whether it is missing or just has the wrong type: either a pointer receiver or wrong signature.

For non\-interface types V, or if static is set, V implements T if all methods of T are present in V. Otherwise \(V is an interface and static is not set\), MissingMethod only checks that methods of T which are also present in V have matching types \(e.g., for a type assertion x.\(T\) where x is of interface type V\).

<a name="NewFunc"></a>

### func [NewFunc](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L408>)

```go
func NewFunc(pos token.Pos, pkg *Package, name string, sig *Signature) *Func
```

NewFunc returns a new function with the given signature, representing the function's type.

<a name="Func.FullName"></a>

### func \(\*Func\) [FullName](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L439>)

```go
func (obj *Func) FullName() string
```

FullName returns the package\- or receiver\-type\-qualified name of function or method obj.

<a name="Func.Origin"></a>

### func \(\*Func\) [Origin](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L457>)

```go
func (obj *Func) Origin() *Func
```

Origin returns the canonical Func for its receiver, i.e. the Func object recorded in Info.Defs.

For synthetic functions created during instantiation \(such as methods on an instantiated Named type or interface methods that depend on type arguments\), this will be the corresponding Func on the generic \(uninstantiated\) type. For all other Funcs Origin returns the receiver.

<a name="Func.Pkg"></a>

### func \(\*Func\) [Pkg](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L468>)

```go
func (obj *Func) Pkg() *Package
```

Pkg returns the package to which the function belongs.

The result is nil for methods of types in the Universe scope, like method Error of the error built\-in interface type.

<a name="Func.Scope"></a>

### func \(\*Func\) [Scope](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L448>)

```go
func (obj *Func) Scope() *Scope
```

Scope returns the scope of the function's body block. The result is nil for imported or instantiated functions and methods \(but there is also no mechanism to get to an instantiated function\).

<a name="Func.Signature"></a>

### func \(\*Func\) [Signature](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L422>)

```go
func (obj *Func) Signature() *Signature
```

Signature returns the signature \(type\) of the function or method.

<a name="Func.String"></a>

### func \(\*Func\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L650>)

```go
func (obj *Func) String() string
```

<a name="ImportMode"></a>

## type [ImportMode](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L95>)

ImportMode is reserved for future use.

```go
type ImportMode int
```

<a name="Importer"></a>

## type [Importer](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L87-L92>)

An Importer resolves import paths to Packages.

CAUTION: This interface does not support the import of locally vendored packages. See https://golang.org/s/go15vendor. If possible, external implementations should implement [ImporterFrom](<#ImporterFrom>).

```go
type Importer interface {
    // Import returns the imported package for the given import path.
    // The semantics is like for ImporterFrom.ImportFrom except that
    // dir and mode are ignored (since they are not present).
    Import(path string) (*Package, error)
}
```

<a name="ImporterFrom"></a>

## type [ImporterFrom](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L100-L118>)

An ImporterFrom resolves import paths to packages; it supports vendoring per https://golang.org/s/go15vendor. Use go/importer to obtain an ImporterFrom implementation.

```go
type ImporterFrom interface {
    // Importer is present for backward-compatibility. Calling
    // Import(path) is the same as calling ImportFrom(path, "", 0);
    // i.e., locally vendored packages may not be found.
    // The types package does not call Import if an ImporterFrom
    // is present.
    Importer

    // ImportFrom returns the imported package for the given import
    // path when imported by a package file located in dir.
    // If the import failed, besides returning an error, ImportFrom
    // is encouraged to cache and return a package anyway, if one
    // was created. This will reduce package inconsistencies and
    // follow-on type checker errors due to the missing package.
    // The mode value must be 0; it is reserved for future use.
    // Two calls to ImportFrom with the same path and dir must
    // return the same package.
    ImportFrom(path, dir string, mode ImportMode) (*Package, error)
}
```

<a name="Info"></a>

## type [Info](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L212-L338>)

Info holds result type information for a type\-checked package. Only the information for which a map is provided is collected. If the package has type errors, the collected information may be incomplete.

```go
type Info struct {
    // Types maps expressions to their types, and for constant
    // expressions, also their values. Invalid expressions are
    // omitted.
    //
    // For (possibly parenthesized) identifiers denoting built-in
    // functions, the recorded signatures are call-site specific:
    // if the call result is not a constant, the recorded type is
    // an argument-specific signature. Otherwise, the recorded type
    // is invalid.
    //
    // The Types map does not record the type of every identifier,
    // only those that appear where an arbitrary expression is
    // permitted. For instance:
    // - an identifier f in a selector expression x.f is found
    //   only in the Selections map;
    // - an identifier z in a variable declaration 'var z int'
    //   is found only in the Defs map;
    // - an identifier p denoting a package in a qualified
    //   identifier p.X is found only in the Uses map.
    //
    // Similarly, no type is recorded for the (synthetic) FuncType
    // node in a FuncDecl.Type field, since there is no corresponding
    // syntactic function type expression in the source in this case
    // Instead, the function type is found in the Defs map entry for
    // the corresponding function declaration.
    Types map[ast.Expr]TypeAndValue

    // Instances maps identifiers denoting generic types or functions to their
    // type arguments and instantiated type.
    //
    // For example, Instances will map the identifier for 'T' in the type
    // instantiation T[int, string] to the type arguments [int, string] and
    // resulting instantiated *Named type. Given a generic function
    // func F[A any](A), Instances will map the identifier for 'F' in the call
    // expression F(int(1)) to the inferred type arguments [int], and resulting
    // instantiated *Signature.
    //
    // Invariant: Instantiating Uses[id].Type() with Instances[id].TypeArgs
    // results in an equivalent of Instances[id].Type.
    Instances map[*ast.Ident]Instance

    // Defs maps identifiers to the objects they define (including
    // package names, dots "." of dot-imports, and blank "_" identifiers).
    // For identifiers that do not denote objects (e.g., the package name
    // in package clauses, or symbolic variables t in t := x.(type) of
    // type switch headers), the corresponding objects are nil.
    //
    // For an embedded field, Defs returns the field *Var it defines.
    //
    // In ill-typed code, such as a duplicate declaration of the
    // same name, Defs may lack an entry for a declaring identifier.
    //
    // Invariant: Defs[id] == nil || Defs[id].Pos() == id.Pos()
    Defs map[*ast.Ident]Object

    // Uses maps identifiers to the objects they denote.
    //
    // For an embedded field, Uses returns the *TypeName it denotes.
    //
    // Invariant: Uses[id].Pos() != id.Pos()
    Uses map[*ast.Ident]Object

    // Implicits maps nodes to their implicitly declared objects, if any.
    // The following node and object types may appear:
    //
    //     node               declared object
    //
    //     *ast.ImportSpec    *PkgName for imports without renames
    //     *ast.CaseClause    type-specific *Var for each type switch case clause (incl. default)
    //     *ast.Field         anonymous parameter *Var (incl. unnamed results)
    //
    Implicits map[ast.Node]Object

    // Selections maps selector expressions (excluding qualified identifiers)
    // to their corresponding selections.
    Selections map[*ast.SelectorExpr]*Selection

    // Scopes maps ast.Nodes to the scopes they define. Package scopes are not
    // associated with a specific node but with all files belonging to a package.
    // Thus, the package scope can be found in the type-checked Package object.
    // Scopes nest, with the Universe scope being the outermost scope, enclosing
    // the package scope, which contains (one or more) files scopes, which enclose
    // function scopes which in turn enclose statement and function literal scopes.
    // Note that even though package-level functions are declared in the package
    // scope, the function scopes are embedded in the file scope of the file
    // containing the function declaration.
    //
    // The Scope of a function contains the declarations of any
    // type parameters, parameters, and named results, plus any
    // local declarations in the body block.
    // It is coextensive with the complete extent of the
    // function's syntax ([*ast.FuncDecl] or [*ast.FuncLit]).
    // The Scopes mapping does not contain an entry for the
    // function body ([*ast.BlockStmt]); the function's scope is
    // associated with the [*ast.FuncType].
    //
    // The following node types may appear in Scopes:
    //
    //     *ast.File
    //     *ast.FuncType
    //     *ast.TypeSpec
    //     *ast.BlockStmt
    //     *ast.IfStmt
    //     *ast.SwitchStmt
    //     *ast.TypeSwitchStmt
    //     *ast.CaseClause
    //     *ast.CommClause
    //     *ast.ForStmt
    //     *ast.RangeStmt
    //
    Scopes map[ast.Node]*Scope

    // InitOrder is the list of package-level initializers in the order in which
    // they must be executed. Initializers referring to variables related by an
    // initialization dependency appear in topological order, the others appear
    // in source order. Variables without an initialization expression do not
    // appear in this list.
    InitOrder []*Initializer

    // FileVersions maps a file to its Go version string.
    // If the file doesn't specify a version, the reported
    // string is Config.GoVersion.
    // Version strings begin with “go”, like “go1.21”, and
    // are suitable for use with the [go/version] package.
    FileVersions map[*ast.File]string
}
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleInfo prints various facts recorded by the type checker in a types.Info struct: definitions of and references to each named object, and the type, value, and mode of every expression in the package.

```go
// Parse a single source file.
const input = `
package fib

type S string

var a, b, c = len(b), S(c), "hello"

func fib(x int) int {
if x < 2 {
	return x
}
return fib(x-1) - fib(x-2)
}`
// We need a specific fileset in this test below for positions.
// Cannot use typecheck helper.
fset := token.NewFileSet()
f := mustParse(fset, input)

// Type-check the package.
// We create an empty map for each kind of input
// we're interested in, and Check populates them.
info := types.Info{
	Types: make(map[ast.Expr]types.TypeAndValue),
	Defs:  make(map[*ast.Ident]types.Object),
	Uses:  make(map[*ast.Ident]types.Object),
}
var conf types.Config
pkg, err := conf.Check("fib", fset, []*ast.File{f}, &info)
if err != nil {
	log.Fatal(err)
}

// Print package-level variables in initialization order.
fmt.Printf("InitOrder: %v\n\n", info.InitOrder)

// For each named object, print the line and
// column of its definition and each of its uses.
fmt.Println("Defs and Uses of each named object:")
usesByObj := make(map[types.Object][]string)
for id, obj := range info.Uses {
	posn := fset.Position(id.Pos())
	lineCol := fmt.Sprintf("%d:%d", posn.Line, posn.Column)
	usesByObj[obj] = append(usesByObj[obj], lineCol)
}
var items []string
for obj, uses := range usesByObj {
	slices.Sort(uses)
	item := fmt.Sprintf("%s:\n  defined at %s\n  used at %s",
		types.ObjectString(obj, types.RelativeTo(pkg)),
		fset.Position(obj.Pos()),
		strings.Join(uses, ", "))
	items = append(items, item)
}
slices.Sort(items) // sort by line:col, in effect
fmt.Println(strings.Join(items, "\n"))
fmt.Println()

fmt.Println("Types and Values of each expression:")
items = nil
for expr, tv := range info.Types {
	var buf strings.Builder
	posn := fset.Position(expr.Pos())
	tvstr := tv.Type.String()
	if tv.Value != nil {
		tvstr += " = " + tv.Value.String()
	}
	// line:col | expr | mode : type = value
	fmt.Fprintf(&buf, "%2d:%2d | %-19s | %-7s : %s",
		posn.Line, posn.Column, exprString(fset, expr),
		mode(tv), tvstr)
	items = append(items, buf.String())
}
slices.Sort(items)
fmt.Println(strings.Join(items, "\n"))

// Output:
// InitOrder: [c = "hello" b = S(c) a = len(b)]
//
// Defs and Uses of each named object:
// builtin len:
//   defined at -
//   used at 6:15
// func fib(x int) int:
//   defined at fib:8:6
//   used at 12:20, 12:9
// type S string:
//   defined at fib:4:6
//   used at 6:23
// type int:
//   defined at -
//   used at 8:12, 8:17
// type string:
//   defined at -
//   used at 4:8
// var b S:
//   defined at fib:6:8
//   used at 6:19
// var c string:
//   defined at fib:6:11
//   used at 6:25
// var x int:
//   defined at fib:8:10
//   used at 10:10, 12:13, 12:24, 9:5
//
// Types and Values of each expression:
//  4: 8 | string              | type    : string
//  6:15 | len                 | builtin : func(fib.S) int
//  6:15 | len(b)              | value   : int
//  6:19 | b                   | var     : fib.S
//  6:23 | S                   | type    : fib.S
//  6:23 | S(c)                | value   : fib.S
//  6:25 | c                   | var     : string
//  6:29 | "hello"             | value   : string = "hello"
//  8:12 | int                 | type    : int
//  8:17 | int                 | type    : int
//  9: 5 | x                   | var     : int
//  9: 5 | x < 2               | value   : untyped bool
//  9: 9 | 2                   | value   : int = 2
// 10:10 | x                   | var     : int
// 12: 9 | fib                 | value   : func(x int) int
// 12: 9 | fib(x - 1)          | value   : int
// 12: 9 | fib(x-1) - fib(x-2) | value   : int
// 12:13 | x                   | var     : int
// 12:13 | x - 1               | value   : int
// 12:15 | 1                   | value   : int = 1
// 12:20 | fib                 | value   : func(x int) int
// 12:20 | fib(x - 2)          | value   : int
// 12:24 | x                   | var     : int
// 12:24 | x - 2               | value   : int
// 12:26 | 2                   | value   : int = 2
```

#### Output

```go
InitOrder: [c = "hello" b = S(c) a = len(b)]

Defs and Uses of each named object:
builtin len:
  defined at -
  used at 6:15
func fib(x int) int:
  defined at fib:8:6
  used at 12:20, 12:9
type S string:
  defined at fib:4:6
  used at 6:23
type int:
  defined at -
  used at 8:12, 8:17
type string:
  defined at -
  used at 4:8
var b S:
  defined at fib:6:8
  used at 6:19
var c string:
  defined at fib:6:11
  used at 6:25
var x int:
  defined at fib:8:10
  used at 10:10, 12:13, 12:24, 9:5

Types and Values of each expression:
 4: 8 | string              | type    : string
 6:15 | len                 | builtin : func(fib.S) int
 6:15 | len(b)              | value   : int
 6:19 | b                   | var     : fib.S
 6:23 | S                   | type    : fib.S
 6:23 | S(c)                | value   : fib.S
 6:25 | c                   | var     : string
 6:29 | "hello"             | value   : string = "hello"
 8:12 | int                 | type    : int
 8:17 | int                 | type    : int
 9: 5 | x                   | var     : int
 9: 5 | x < 2               | value   : untyped bool
 9: 9 | 2                   | value   : int = 2
10:10 | x                   | var     : int
12: 9 | fib                 | value   : func(x int) int
12: 9 | fib(x - 1)          | value   : int
12: 9 | fib(x-1) - fib(x-2) | value   : int
12:13 | x                   | var     : int
12:13 | x - 1               | value   : int
12:15 | 1                   | value   : int = 1
12:20 | fib                 | value   : func(x int) int
12:20 | fib(x - 2)          | value   : int
12:24 | x                   | var     : int
12:24 | x - 2               | value   : int
12:26 | 2                   | value   : int = 2
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Info.ObjectOf"></a>

### func \(\*Info\) [ObjectOf](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L365>)

```go
func (info *Info) ObjectOf(id *ast.Ident) Object
```

ObjectOf returns the object denoted by the specified id, or nil if not found.

If id is an embedded struct field, [Info.ObjectOf](<#Info.ObjectOf>) returns the field \(\*[Var](<#Var>)\) it defines, not the type \(\*[TypeName](<#TypeName>)\) it uses.

Precondition: the Uses and Defs maps are populated.

<a name="Info.PkgNameOf"></a>

### func \(\*Info\) [PkgNameOf](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L378>)

```go
func (info *Info) PkgNameOf(imp *ast.ImportSpec) *PkgName
```

PkgNameOf returns the local package name defined by the import, or nil if not found.

For dot\-imports, the package name is ".".

Precondition: the Defs and Implicts maps are populated.

<a name="Info.TypeOf"></a>

### func \(\*Info\) [TypeOf](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L346>)

```go
func (info *Info) TypeOf(e ast.Expr) Type
```

TypeOf returns the type of expression e, or nil if not found. Precondition: the Types, Uses and Defs maps are populated.

<a name="Initializer"></a>

## type [Initializer](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L461-L464>)

An Initializer describes a package\-level variable, or a list of variables in case of a multi\-valued initialization expression, and the corresponding initialization expression.

```go
type Initializer struct {
    Lhs []*Var // var Lhs = Rhs
    Rhs ast.Expr
}
```

<a name="Initializer.String"></a>

### func \(\*Initializer\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L466>)

```go
func (init *Initializer) String() string
```

<a name="Instance"></a>

## type [Instance](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L453-L456>)

Instance reports the type arguments and instantiated type for type and function instantiations. For type instantiations, [Type](<#Type>) will be of dynamic type \*[Named](<#Named>). For function instantiations, [Type](<#Type>) will be of dynamic type \*Signature.

```go
type Instance struct {
    TypeArgs *TypeList
    Type     Type
}
```

<a name="Interface"></a>

## type [Interface](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L17-L26>)

An Interface represents an interface type.

```go
type Interface struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewInterface"></a>

### func [NewInterface](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L39>)

```go
func NewInterface(methods []*Func, embeddeds []*Named) *Interface
```

NewInterface returns a new interface for the given methods and embedded types. NewInterface takes ownership of the provided methods and may modify their types by setting missing receivers.

Deprecated: Use NewInterfaceType instead which allows arbitrary embedded types.

<a name="NewInterfaceType"></a>

### func [NewInterfaceType](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L53>)

```go
func NewInterfaceType(methods []*Func, embeddeds []Type) *Interface
```

NewInterfaceType returns a new interface for the given methods and embedded types. NewInterfaceType takes ownership of the provided methods and may modify their types by setting missing receivers.

To avoid race conditions, the interface's type set should be computed before concurrent use of the interface, by explicitly calling Complete.

<a name="Interface.Complete"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [Complete](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L139>)

```go
func (t *Interface) Complete() *Interface
```

Complete computes the interface's type set. It must be called by users of [NewInterfaceType](<#NewInterfaceType>) and [NewInterface](<#NewInterface>) after the interface's embedded types are fully defined and before using the interface type in any way other than to form other types. The interface must not contain duplicate methods or a panic occurs. Complete returns the receiver.

Interface types that have been completed are safe for concurrent use.

<a name="Interface.Embedded"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [Embedded](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L107>)

```go
func (t *Interface) Embedded(i int) *Named
```

Embedded returns the i'th embedded defined \(\*[Named](<#Named>)\) type of interface t for 0 \<= i \< t.NumEmbeddeds\(\). The result is nil if the i'th embedded type is not a defined type.

Deprecated: Use [Interface.EmbeddedType](<#Interface.EmbeddedType>) which is not restricted to defined \(\*[Named](<#Named>)\) types.

<a name="Interface.EmbeddedType"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [EmbeddedType](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L110>)

```go
func (t *Interface) EmbeddedType(i int) Type
```

EmbeddedType returns the i'th embedded type of interface t for 0 \<= i \< t.NumEmbeddeds\(\).

<a name="Interface.EmbeddedTypes"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [EmbeddedTypes](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/iter.go#L47>)

```go
func (t *Interface) EmbeddedTypes() iter.Seq[Type]
```

EmbeddedTypes returns a go1.23 iterator over the types embedded within an interface.

Example: for e := range t.EmbeddedTypes\(\) \{ ... \}

<a name="Interface.Empty"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [Empty](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L120>)

```go
func (t *Interface) Empty() bool
```

Empty reports whether t is the empty interface.

<a name="Interface.ExplicitMethod"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [ExplicitMethod](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L98>)

```go
func (t *Interface) ExplicitMethod(i int) *Func
```

ExplicitMethod returns the i'th explicitly declared method of interface t for 0 \<= i \< t.NumExplicitMethods\(\). The methods are ordered by their unique [Id](<#Id>).

<a name="Interface.ExplicitMethods"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [ExplicitMethods](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/iter.go#L34>)

```go
func (t *Interface) ExplicitMethods() iter.Seq[*Func]
```

ExplicitMethods returns a go1.23 iterator over the explicit methods of an interface, ordered by Id.

Example: for m := range t.ExplicitMethods\(\) \{ ... \}

<a name="Interface.IsComparable"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [IsComparable](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L123>)

```go
func (t *Interface) IsComparable() bool
```

IsComparable reports whether each type in interface t's type set is comparable.

<a name="Interface.IsImplicit"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [IsImplicit](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L130>)

```go
func (t *Interface) IsImplicit() bool
```

IsImplicit reports whether the interface t is a wrapper for a type set literal.

<a name="Interface.IsMethodSet"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [IsMethodSet](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L127>)

```go
func (t *Interface) IsMethodSet() bool
```

IsMethodSet reports whether the interface t is fully described by its method set.

<a name="Interface.MarkImplicit"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [MarkImplicit](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L89>)

```go
func (t *Interface) MarkImplicit()
```

MarkImplicit marks the interface t as implicit, meaning this interface corresponds to a constraint literal such as \~T or A|B without explicit interface embedding. MarkImplicit should be called before any concurrent use of implicit interfaces.

<a name="Interface.Method"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [Method](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L117>)

```go
func (t *Interface) Method(i int) *Func
```

Method returns the i'th method of interface t for 0 \<= i \< t.NumMethods\(\). The methods are ordered by their unique Id.

<a name="Interface.Methods"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [Methods](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/iter.go#L20>)

```go
func (t *Interface) Methods() iter.Seq[*Func]
```

Methods returns a go1.23 iterator over all the methods of an interface, ordered by Id.

Example: for m := range t.Methods\(\) \{ ... \}

<a name="Interface.NumEmbeddeds"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [NumEmbeddeds](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L101>)

```go
func (t *Interface) NumEmbeddeds() int
```

NumEmbeddeds returns the number of embedded types in interface t.

<a name="Interface.NumExplicitMethods"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [NumExplicitMethods](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L94>)

```go
func (t *Interface) NumExplicitMethods() int
```

NumExplicitMethods returns the number of explicitly declared methods of interface t.

<a name="Interface.NumMethods"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [NumMethods](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L113>)

```go
func (t *Interface) NumMethods() int
```

NumMethods returns the total number of methods of interface t.

<a name="Interface.String"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L148>)

```go
func (t *Interface) String() string
```

<a name="Interface.Underlying"></a>

### func \(\*Interface\) [Underlying](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/interface.go#L147>)

```go
func (t *Interface) Underlying() Type
```



<a name="Map.Key"></a>

### func \(\*Map\) [Key](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/map.go#L21>)

```go
func (m *Map) Key() Type
```

<a name="Map.String"></a>

