<a name="MethodSet"></a>

## type [MethodSet](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/methodset.go#L18-L20>)

A MethodSet is an ordered set of concrete or abstract \(interface\) methods; a method is a [MethodVal](<#FieldVal>) selection, and they are ordered by ascending m.Obj\(\).Id\(\). The zero value for a MethodSet is a ready\-to\-use empty method set.

```go
type MethodSet struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleMethodSet prints the method sets of various types.

```go
// Parse a single source file.
const input = `
package temperature
import "fmt"
type Celsius float64
func (c Celsius) String() string  { return fmt.Sprintf("%g°C", c) }
func (c *Celsius) SetF(f float64) { *c = Celsius(f - 32 / 9 * 5) }

type S struct { I; m int }
type I interface { m() byte }
`
fset := token.NewFileSet()
f, err := parser.ParseFile(fset, "celsius.go", input, 0)
if err != nil {
	log.Fatal(err)
}

// Type-check a package consisting of this file.
// Type information for the imported packages
// comes from $GOROOT/pkg/$GOOS_$GOOARCH/fmt.a.
conf := types.Config{Importer: defaultImporter(fset)}
pkg, err := conf.Check("temperature", fset, []*ast.File{f}, nil)
if err != nil {
	log.Fatal(err)
}

// Print the method sets of Celsius and *Celsius.
celsius := pkg.Scope().Lookup("Celsius").Type()
for _, t := range []types.Type{celsius, types.NewPointer(celsius)} {
	fmt.Printf("Method set of %s:\n", t)
	for m := range types.NewMethodSet(t).Methods() {
		fmt.Println(m)
	}
	fmt.Println()
}

// Print the method set of S.
styp := pkg.Scope().Lookup("S").Type()
fmt.Printf("Method set of %s:\n", styp)
fmt.Println(types.NewMethodSet(styp))

// Output:
// Method set of temperature.Celsius:
// method (temperature.Celsius) String() string
//
// Method set of *temperature.Celsius:
// method (*temperature.Celsius) SetF(f float64)
// method (*temperature.Celsius) String() string
//
// Method set of temperature.S:
// MethodSet {}
```

#### Output

```go
Method set of temperature.Celsius:
method (temperature.Celsius) String() string

Method set of *temperature.Celsius:
method (*temperature.Celsius) SetF(f float64)
method (*temperature.Celsius) String() string

Method set of temperature.S:
MethodSet {}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewMethodSet"></a>

### func [NewMethodSet](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/methodset.go#L72>)

```go
func NewMethodSet(T Type) *MethodSet
```

NewMethodSet returns the method set for the given type T. It always returns a non\-nil method set, even if it is empty.

<a name="MethodSet.At"></a>

### func \(\*MethodSet\) [At](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/methodset.go#L40>)

```go
func (s *MethodSet) At(i int) *Selection
```

At returns the i'th method in s for 0 \<= i \< s.Len\(\).

<a name="MethodSet.Len"></a>

### func \(\*MethodSet\) [Len](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/methodset.go#L37>)

```go
func (s *MethodSet) Len() int
```

Len returns the number of methods in s.

<a name="MethodSet.Lookup"></a>

### func \(\*MethodSet\) [Lookup](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/methodset.go#L43>)

```go
func (s *MethodSet) Lookup(pkg *Package, name string) *Selection
```

Lookup returns the method with matching package and name, or nil if not found.

<a name="MethodSet.Methods"></a>

### func \(\*MethodSet\) [Methods](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/iter.go#L112>)

```go
func (s *MethodSet) Methods() iter.Seq[*Selection]
```

Methods returns a go1.23 iterator over the methods of a method set.

Example: for method := range s.Methods\(\) \{ ... \}

<a name="MethodSet.String"></a>

### func \(\*MethodSet\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/methodset.go#L22>)

```go
func (s *MethodSet) String() string
```

<a name="Named"></a>

## type [Named](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/named.go#L108-L134>)

A Named represents a named \(defined\) type.

A declaration such as:

```go
type S struct { ... }
```

creates a defined type whose underlying type is a struct, and binds this type to the object S, a [TypeName](<#TypeName>). Use [Named.Underlying](<#Named.Underlying>) to access the underlying type. Use [Named.Obj](<#Named.Obj>) to obtain the object S.

Before type aliases \(Go 1.9\), the spec called defined types "named types".

```go
type Named struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewNamed"></a>

### func [NewNamed](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/named.go#L191>)

```go
func NewNamed(obj *TypeName, underlying Type, methods []*Func) *Named
```

NewNamed returns a new named type for the given type name, underlying type, and associated methods. If the given type name obj doesn't have a type yet, its type is set to the returned named type. The underlying type must not be a \*Named.

<a name="Named.AddMethod"></a>

### func \(\*Named\) [AddMethod](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/named.go#L545>)

```go
func (t *Named) AddMethod(m *Func)
```

AddMethod adds method m unless it is already in the method list. The method must be in the same package as t, and t must not have type arguments.

<a name="Named.Method"></a>

### func \(\*Named\) [Method](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/named.go#L426>)

```go
func (t *Named) Method(i int) *Func
```

Method returns the i'th method of named type t for 0 \<= i \< t.NumMethods\(\).

For an ordinary or instantiated type t, the receiver base type of this method is the named type t. For an uninstantiated generic type t, each method receiver is instantiated with its receiver type parameters.

Methods are numbered deterministically: given the same list of source files presented to the type checker, or the same sequence of NewMethod and AddMethod calls, the mapping from method index to corresponding method remains the same. But the specific ordering is not specified and must not be relied on as it may change in the future.

<a name="Named.Methods"></a>

### func \(\*Named\) [Methods](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/iter.go#L60>)

```go
func (t *Named) Methods() iter.Seq[*Func]
```

Methods returns a go1.23 iterator over the declared methods of a named type.

Example: for m := range t.Methods\(\) \{ ... \}

<a name="Named.NumMethods"></a>

### func \(\*Named\) [NumMethods](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/named.go#L411>)

```go
func (t *Named) NumMethods() int
```

NumMethods returns the number of explicit methods defined for t.

<a name="Named.Obj"></a>

### func \(\*Named\) [Obj](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/named.go#L375>)

```go
func (t *Named) Obj() *TypeName
```

Obj returns the type name for the declaration defining the named type t. For instantiated types, this is same as the type name of the origin type.

<a name="Named.Origin"></a>

### func \(\*Named\) [Origin](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/named.go#L384>)

```go
func (t *Named) Origin() *Named
```

Origin returns the generic type from which the named type t is instantiated. If t is not an instantiated type, the result is t.

<a name="Named.SetTypeParams"></a>

### func \(\*Named\) [SetTypeParams](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/named.go#L397>)

```go
func (t *Named) SetTypeParams(tparams []*TypeParam)
```

SetTypeParams sets the type parameters of the named type t. t must not have type arguments.

<a name="Named.SetUnderlying"></a>

### func \(\*Named\) [SetUnderlying](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/named.go#L521>)

```go
func (t *Named) SetUnderlying(u Type)
```

SetUnderlying sets the underlying type and marks t as complete. t must not have type arguments.

<a name="Named.String"></a>

### func \(\*Named\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/named.go#L612>)

```go
func (t *Named) String() string
```

<a name="Named.TypeArgs"></a>

### func \(\*Named\) [TypeArgs](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/named.go#L403>)

```go
func (t *Named) TypeArgs() *TypeList
```

TypeArgs returns the type arguments used to instantiate the named type t.

<a name="Named.TypeParams"></a>

### func \(\*Named\) [TypeParams](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/named.go#L393>)

```go
func (t *Named) TypeParams() *TypeParamList
```

TypeParams returns the type parameters of the named type t, or nil. The result is non\-nil for an \(originally\) generic type even if it is instantiated.

<a name="Named.Underlying"></a>

### func \(\*Named\) [Underlying](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/named.go#L592>)

```go
func (n *Named) Underlying() Type
```

Underlying returns the [underlying type](<https://go.dev/ref/spec#Underlying_types.>) of the named type t, resolving all forwarding declarations. Underlying types are never Named, TypeParam, or Alias types.

<a name="Nil"></a>

## type [Nil](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L515-L517>)

Nil represents the predeclared value nil.

```go
type Nil struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Nil.String"></a>

### func \(\*Nil\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L653>)

```go
func (obj *Nil) String() string
```

<a name="Object"></a>

## type [Object](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L29-L67>)

An Object is a named language entity. An Object may be a constant \([Const](<#Const>)\), type name \([TypeName](<#TypeName>)\), variable or struct field \([Var](<#Var>)\), function or method \([Func](<#Func>)\), imported package \([PkgName](<#PkgName>)\), label \([Label](<#Label>)\), built\-in function \([Builtin](<#Builtin>)\), or the predeclared identifier 'nil' \([Nil](<#Nil>)\).

The environment, which is structured as a tree of Scopes, maps each name to the unique Object that it denotes.

```go
type Object interface {
    Parent() *Scope // scope in which this object is declared; nil for methods and struct fields
    Pos() token.Pos // position of object identifier in declaration
    Pkg() *Package  // package to which this object belongs; nil for labels and objects in the Universe scope
    Name() string   // package local object name
    Type() Type     // object type
    Exported() bool // reports whether the name starts with a capital letter
    Id() string     // object name if exported, qualified name if not exported (see func Id)

    // String returns a human-readable string of the object.
    // Use [ObjectString] to control how package names are formatted in the string.
    String() string
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

<a name="LookupFieldOrMethod"></a>

### func [LookupFieldOrMethod](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/lookup.go#L85>)

```go
func LookupFieldOrMethod(T Type, addressable bool, pkg *Package, name string) (obj Object, index []int, indirect bool)
```

LookupFieldOrMethod looks up a field or method with given package and name in T and returns the corresponding \*Var or \*Func, an index sequence, and a bool indicating if there were any pointer indirections on the path to the field or method. If addressable is set, T is the type of an addressable variable \(only matters for method lookups\). T must not be nil.

The last index entry is the field or method index in the \(possibly embedded\) type where the entry was found, either:

1. the list of declared methods of a named type; or
2. the list of all methods \(method set\) of an interface type; or
3. the list of fields of a struct type.

The earlier index entries are the indices of the embedded struct fields traversed to get to the found entry, starting at depth 0.

If no entry is found, a nil object is returned. In this case, the returned index and indirect values have the following meaning:

- If index \!= nil, the index sequence points to an ambiguous entry \(the same name appeared more than once at the same embedding level\).

- If indirect is set, a method with a pointer receiver type was found but there was no pointer on the path from the actual receiver type to the method's formal receiver base type, nor was the receiver addressable.

See also [LookupSelection](<#LookupSelection>), which returns the result as a [Selection](<#Selection>).

<a name="Package"></a>

## type [Package](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/package.go#L15-L24>)

A Package describes a Go package.

```go
type Package struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Unsafe"></a>The Unsafe package is the package returned by an importer for the import path "unsafe".

```go
var Unsafe *Package
```

<a name="NewPackage"></a>

### func [NewPackage](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/package.go#L28>)

```go
func NewPackage(path, name string) *Package
```

NewPackage returns a new Package for the given package path and name. The package is not complete and contains no explicit imports.

<a name="Package.Complete"></a>

### func \(\*Package\) [Complete](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/package.go#L61>)

```go
func (pkg *Package) Complete() bool
```

A package is complete if its scope contains \(at least\) all exported objects; otherwise it is incomplete.

<a name="Package.GoVersion"></a>

### func \(\*Package\) [GoVersion](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/package.go#L46>)

```go
func (pkg *Package) GoVersion() string
```

GoVersion returns the minimum Go version required by this package. If the minimum version is unknown, GoVersion returns the empty string. Individual source files may specify a different minimum Go version, as reported in the [go/ast.File.GoVersion](<https://pkg.go.dev/go/ast/#File.GoVersion>) field.

<a name="Package.Imports"></a>

### func \(\*Package\) [Imports](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/package.go#L75>)

```go
func (pkg *Package) Imports() []*Package
```

Imports returns the list of packages directly imported by pkg; the list is in source order.

If pkg was loaded from export data, Imports includes packages that provide package\-level objects referenced by pkg. This may be more or less than the set of packages directly imported by pkg's source code.

If pkg uses cgo and the FakeImportC configuration option was enabled, the imports list may contain a fake "C" package.

<a name="Package.MarkComplete"></a>

### func \(\*Package\) [MarkComplete](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/package.go#L64>)

```go
func (pkg *Package) MarkComplete()
```

MarkComplete marks a package as complete.

<a name="Package.Name"></a>

### func \(\*Package\) [Name](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/package.go#L37>)

```go
func (pkg *Package) Name() string
```

Name returns the package name.

<a name="Package.Path"></a>

### func \(\*Package\) [Path](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/package.go#L34>)

```go
func (pkg *Package) Path() string
```

Path returns the package path.

<a name="Package.Scope"></a>

### func \(\*Package\) [Scope](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/package.go#L52>)

```go
func (pkg *Package) Scope() *Scope
```

Scope returns the \(complete or incomplete\) package scope holding the objects declared at package level \(TypeNames, Consts, Vars, and Funcs\). For a nil pkg receiver, Scope returns the Universe scope.

<a name="Package.SetImports"></a>

### func \(\*Package\) [SetImports](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/package.go#L79>)

```go
func (pkg *Package) SetImports(list []*Package)
```

SetImports sets the list of explicitly imported packages to list. It is the caller's responsibility to make sure list elements are unique.

<a name="Package.SetName"></a>

### func \(\*Package\) [SetName](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/package.go#L40>)

```go
func (pkg *Package) SetName(name string)
```

SetName sets the package name.

<a name="Package.String"></a>

### func \(\*Package\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/package.go#L81>)

```go
func (pkg *Package) String() string
```

<a name="PkgName"></a>

## type [PkgName](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L205-L208>)

A PkgName represents an imported Go package. PkgNames don't have a type.

```go
type PkgName struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewPkgName"></a>

### func [NewPkgName](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L212>)

```go
func NewPkgName(pos token.Pos, pkg *Package, name string, imported *Package) *PkgName
```

NewPkgName returns a new PkgName object representing an imported package. The remaining arguments set the attributes found with all Objects.

<a name="PkgName.Imported"></a>

### func \(\*PkgName\) [Imported](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L218>)

```go
func (obj *PkgName) Imported() *Package
```

Imported returns the package that was imported. It is distinct from Pkg\(\), which is the package containing the import statement.

<a name="PkgName.String"></a>

### func \(\*PkgName\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L646>)

```go
func (obj *PkgName) String() string
```

<a name="Pointer"></a>

## type [Pointer](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/pointer.go#L11-L13>)

A Pointer represents a pointer type.

```go
type Pointer struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewPointer"></a>

### func [NewPointer](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/pointer.go#L16>)

```go
func NewPointer(elem Type) *Pointer
```

NewPointer returns a new pointer type for the given element \(base\) type.

<a name="Pointer.Elem"></a>

### func \(\*Pointer\) [Elem](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/pointer.go#L19>)

```go
func (p *Pointer) Elem() Type
```

Elem returns the element type for the given pointer p.

<a name="Pointer.String"></a>

### func \(\*Pointer\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/pointer.go#L22>)

```go
func (p *Pointer) String() string
```

<a name="Pointer.Underlying"></a>

### func \(\*Pointer\) [Underlying](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/pointer.go#L21>)

```go
func (p *Pointer) Underlying() Type
```

<a name="Qualifier"></a>

## type [Qualifier](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/typestring.go#L31>)

A Qualifier controls how named package\-level objects are printed in calls to [TypeString](<#TypeString>), [ObjectString](<#ObjectString>), and [SelectionString](<#SelectionString>).

These three formatting routines call the Qualifier for each package\-level object O, and if the Qualifier returns a non\-empty string p, the object is printed in the form p.O. If it returns an empty string, only the object name O is printed.

Using a nil Qualifier is equivalent to using \(\*[Package](<#Package>)\).Path: the object is qualified by the import path, e.g., "encoding/json.Marshal".

```go
type Qualifier func(*Package) string
```

<a name="RelativeTo"></a>

### func [RelativeTo](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/typestring.go#L35>)

```go
func RelativeTo(pkg *Package) Qualifier
```

RelativeTo returns a [Qualifier](<#Qualifier>) that fully qualifies members of all packages other than pkg.

<a name="Scope"></a>

## type [Scope](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope.go#L25-L33>)

A Scope maintains a set of objects and links to its containing \(parent\) and contained \(children\) scopes. Objects may be inserted and looked up by name. The zero value for Scope is a ready\-to\-use empty scope.

```go
type Scope struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Universe"></a>The Universe scope contains all predeclared objects of Go. It is the outermost scope of any chain of nested scopes.

```go
var Universe *Scope
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleScope prints the tree of Scopes of a package created from a set of parsed files.

```go
// Parse the source files for a package.
fset := token.NewFileSet()
var files []*ast.File
for _, src := range []string{
	`package main
import "fmt"
func main() {
freezing := FToC(-18)
fmt.Println(freezing, Boiling) }
`,
	`package main
import "fmt"
type Celsius float64
func (c Celsius) String() string { return fmt.Sprintf("%g°C", c) }
func FToC(f float64) Celsius { return Celsius(f - 32 / 9 * 5) }
const Boiling Celsius = 100
func Unused() { {}; {{ var x int; _ = x }} } // make sure empty block scopes get printed
`,
} {
	files = append(files, mustParse(fset, src))
}

// Type-check a package consisting of these files.
// Type information for the imported "fmt" package
// comes from $GOROOT/pkg/$GOOS_$GOOARCH/fmt.a.
conf := types.Config{Importer: defaultImporter(fset)}
pkg, err := conf.Check("temperature", fset, files, nil)
if err != nil {
	log.Fatal(err)
}

// Print the tree of scopes.
// For determinism, we redact addresses.
var buf strings.Builder
pkg.Scope().WriteTo(&buf, 0, true)
rx := regexp.MustCompile(` 0x[a-fA-F\d]*`)
fmt.Println(rx.ReplaceAllString(buf.String(), ""))

// Output:
// package "temperature" scope {
// .  const temperature.Boiling temperature.Celsius
// .  type temperature.Celsius float64
// .  func temperature.FToC(f float64) temperature.Celsius
// .  func temperature.Unused()
// .  func temperature.main()
// .  main scope {
// .  .  package fmt
// .  .  function scope {
// .  .  .  var freezing temperature.Celsius
// .  .  }
// .  }
// .  main scope {
// .  .  package fmt
// .  .  function scope {
// .  .  .  var c temperature.Celsius
// .  .  }
// .  .  function scope {
// .  .  .  var f float64
// .  .  }
// .  .  function scope {
// .  .  .  block scope {
// .  .  .  }
// .  .  .  block scope {
// .  .  .  .  block scope {
// .  .  .  .  .  var x int
// .  .  .  .  }
// .  .  .  }
// .  .  }
// .  }
// }
```

#### Output

```go
package "temperature" scope {
.  const temperature.Boiling temperature.Celsius
.  type temperature.Celsius float64
.  func temperature.FToC(f float64) temperature.Celsius
.  func temperature.Unused()
.  func temperature.main()
.  main scope {
.  .  package fmt
.  .  function scope {
.  .  .  var freezing temperature.Celsius
.  .  }
.  }
.  main scope {
.  .  package fmt
.  .  function scope {
.  .  .  var c temperature.Celsius
.  .  }
.  .  function scope {
.  .  .  var f float64
.  .  }
.  .  function scope {
.  .  .  block scope {
.  .  .  }
.  .  .  block scope {
.  .  .  .  block scope {
.  .  .  .  .  var x int
.  .  .  .  }
.  .  .  }
.  .  }
.  }
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="NewScope"></a>

### func [NewScope](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope.go#L37>)

```go
func NewScope(parent *Scope, pos, end token.Pos, comment string) *Scope
```

NewScope returns a new, empty scope contained in the given parent scope, if any. The comment is for debugging only.

<a name="Scope.Child"></a>

### func \(\*Scope\) [Child](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope.go#L69>)

```go
func (s *Scope) Child(i int) *Scope
```

Child returns the i'th child scope for 0 \<= i \< NumChildren\(\).

<a name="Scope.Children"></a>

### func \(\*Scope\) [Children](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/iter.go#L73>)

```go
func (s *Scope) Children() iter.Seq[*Scope]
```

Children returns a go1.23 iterator over the child scopes nested within scope s.

Example: for child := range scope.Children\(\) \{ ... \}

<a name="Scope.Contains"></a>

### func \(\*Scope\) [Contains](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope2.go#L43>)

```go
func (s *Scope) Contains(pos token.Pos) bool
```

Contains reports whether pos is within the scope's extent. The result is guaranteed to be valid only if the type\-checked AST has complete position information.

<a name="Scope.End"></a>

### func \(\*Scope\) [End](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope2.go#L38>)

```go
func (s *Scope) End() token.Pos
```

<a name="Scope.Innermost"></a>

### func \(\*Scope\) [Innermost](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope2.go#L52>)

```go
func (s *Scope) Innermost(pos token.Pos) *Scope
```

Innermost returns the innermost \(child\) scope containing pos. If pos is not within any scope, the result is nil. The result is also nil for the Universe scope. The result is guaranteed to be valid only if the type\-checked AST has complete position information.

<a name="Scope.Insert"></a>

### func \(\*Scope\) [Insert](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope.go#L107>)

```go
func (s *Scope) Insert(obj Object) Object
```

Insert attempts to insert an object obj into scope s. If s already contains an alternative object alt with the same name, Insert leaves s unchanged and returns alt. Otherwise it inserts obj, sets the object's parent scope if not already set, and returns nil.

<a name="Scope.Len"></a>

### func \(\*Scope\) [Len](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope.go#L51>)

```go
func (s *Scope) Len() int
```

Len returns the number of scope elements.

<a name="Scope.Lookup"></a>

### func \(\*Scope\) [Lookup](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope.go#L73>)

```go
func (s *Scope) Lookup(name string) Object
```

Lookup returns the object in scope s with the given name if such an object exists; otherwise the result is nil.

<a name="Scope.LookupParent"></a>

### func \(\*Scope\) [LookupParent](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope2.go#L24>)

```go
func (s *Scope) LookupParent(name string, pos token.Pos) (*Scope, Object)
```

LookupParent follows the parent chain of scopes starting with s until it finds a scope where Lookup\(name\) returns a non\-nil object, and then returns that scope and object. If a valid position pos is provided, only objects that were declared at or before pos are considered. If no such scope and object exists, the result is \(nil, nil\). The results are guaranteed to be valid only if the type\-checked AST has complete position information.

Note that obj.Parent\(\) may be different from the returned scope if the object was inserted into the scope and already had a parent at that time \(see Insert\). This can only happen for dot\-imported objects whose parent is the scope of the package that exported them.

<a name="Scope.Names"></a>

### func \(\*Scope\) [Names](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope.go#L54>)

```go
func (s *Scope) Names() []string
```

Names returns the scope's element names in sorted order.

<a name="Scope.NumChildren"></a>

### func \(\*Scope\) [NumChildren](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope.go#L66>)

```go
func (s *Scope) NumChildren() int
```

NumChildren returns the number of scopes nested in s.

<a name="Scope.Parent"></a>

### func \(\*Scope\) [Parent](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope.go#L48>)

```go
func (s *Scope) Parent() *Scope
```

Parent returns the scope's containing \(parent\) scope.

<a name="Scope.Pos"></a>

### func \(\*Scope\) [Pos](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope2.go#L37>)

```go
func (s *Scope) Pos() token.Pos
```

Pos and End describe the scope's source code extent \[pos, end\). The results are guaranteed to be valid only if the type\-checked AST has complete position information. The extent is undefined for Universe and package scopes.

<a name="Scope.String"></a>

### func \(\*Scope\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope.go#L172>)

```go
func (s *Scope) String() string
```

String returns a string representation of the scope, for debugging.

<a name="Scope.WriteTo"></a>

### func \(\*Scope\) [WriteTo](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/scope.go#L151>)

```go
func (s *Scope) WriteTo(w io.Writer, n int, recurse bool)
```

WriteTo writes a string representation of the scope to w, with the scope elements sorted by name. The level of indentation is controlled by n \>= 0, with n == 0 for no indentation. If recurse is set, it also writes nested \(children\) scopes.

<a name="Selection"></a>

## type [Selection](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/selection.go#L75-L81>)

A Selection describes a selector expression x.f. For the declarations:

```go
type T struct{ x int; E }
type E struct{}
func (e E) m() {}
var p *T
```

the following relations exist:

```go
Selector    Kind          Recv    Obj    Type       Index     Indirect

p.x         FieldVal      T       x      int        {0}       true
p.m         MethodVal     *T      m      func()     {1, 0}    true
T.m         MethodExpr    T       m      func(T)    {1, 0}    false
```

```go
type Selection struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="LookupSelection"></a>

### func [LookupSelection](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/lookup.go#L37>)

```go
func LookupSelection(T Type, addressable bool, pkg *Package, name string) (Selection, bool)
```

LookupSelection selects the field or method whose ID is Id\(pkg, name\), on a value of type T. If addressable is set, T is the type of an addressable variable \(this matters only for method lookups\). T must not be nil.

If the selection is valid:

- [Selection.Obj](<#Selection.Obj>) returns the field \([Var](<#Var>)\) or method \([Func](<#Func>)\);
- [Selection.Indirect](<#Selection.Indirect>) reports whether there were any pointer indirections on the path to the field or method.
- [Selection.Index](<#Selection.Index>) returns the index sequence, defined below.

The last index entry is the field or method index in the \(possibly embedded\) type where the entry was found, either:

1. the list of declared methods of a named type; or
2. the list of all methods \(method set\) of an interface type; or
3. the list of fields of a struct type.

The earlier index entries are the indices of the embedded struct fields traversed to get to the found entry, starting at depth 0.

See also [LookupFieldOrMethod](<#LookupFieldOrMethod>), which returns the components separately.

<a name="Selection.Index"></a>

### func \(\*Selection\) [Index](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/selection.go#L137>)

```go
func (s *Selection) Index() []int
```

Index describes the path from x to f in x.f. The last index entry is the field or method index of the type declaring f; either:

1. the list of declared methods of a named type; or
2. the list of methods of an interface type; or
3. the list of fields of a struct type.

The earlier index entries are the indices of the embedded fields implicitly traversed to get from \(the type of\) x to f, starting at embedding depth 0.

<a name="Selection.Indirect"></a>

### func \(\*Selection\) [Indirect](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/selection.go#L146>)

```go
func (s *Selection) Indirect() bool
```

Indirect reports whether any pointer indirection was required to get from x to f in x.f.

Beware: Indirect spuriously returns true \(Go issue \#8353\) for a MethodVal selection in which the receiver argument and parameter both have type \*T so there is no indirection. Unfortunately, a fix is too risky.

<a name="Selection.Kind"></a>

### func \(\*Selection\) [Kind](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/selection.go#L84>)

```go
func (s *Selection) Kind() SelectionKind
```

Kind returns the selection kind.

<a name="Selection.Obj"></a>

### func \(\*Selection\) [Obj](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/selection.go#L91>)

```go
func (s *Selection) Obj() Object
```

Obj returns the object denoted by x.f; a \*Var for a field selection, and a \*Func in all other cases.

<a name="Selection.Recv"></a>

### func \(\*Selection\) [Recv](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/selection.go#L87>)

```go
func (s *Selection) Recv() Type
```

Recv returns the type of x in x.f.

<a name="Selection.String"></a>

### func \(\*Selection\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/selection.go#L148>)

```go
func (s *Selection) String() string
```

<a name="Selection.Type"></a>

### func \(\*Selection\) [Type](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/selection.go#L95>)

```go
func (s *Selection) Type() Type
```

Type returns the type of x.f, which may be different from the type of f. See Selection for more information.

<a name="SelectionKind"></a>

## type [SelectionKind](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/selection.go#L52>)

SelectionKind describes the kind of a selector expression x.f \(excluding qualified identifiers\).

If x is a struct or \*struct, a selector expression x.f may denote a sequence of selection operations x.a.b.c.f. The SelectionKind describes the kind of the final \(explicit\) operation; all the previous \(implicit\) operations are always field selections. Each element of Indices specifies an implicit field \(a, b, c\) by its index in the struct type of the field selection operand.

For a FieldVal operation, the final selection refers to the field specified by Selection.Obj.

For a MethodVal operation, the final selection refers to a method. If the "pointerness" of the method's declared receiver does not match that of the effective receiver after implicit field selection, then an & or \* operation is implicitly applied to the receiver variable or value. So, x.f denotes \(&x.a.b.c\).f when f requires a pointer receiver but x.a.b.c is a non\-pointer variable; and it denotes \(\*x.a.b.c\).f when f requires a non\-pointer receiver but x.a.b.c is a pointer value.

All pointer indirections, whether due to implicit or explicit field selections or \* operations inserted for "pointerness", panic if applied to a nil pointer, so a method call x.f\(\) may panic even before the function call.

By contrast, a MethodExpr operation T.f is essentially equivalent to a function literal of the form:

```go
func(x T, args) (results) { return x.f(args) }
```

Consequently, any implicit field selections and \* operations inserted for "pointerness" are not evaluated until the function is called, so a T.f or \(\*T\).f expression never panics.

```go
type SelectionKind int
```

<a name="FieldVal"></a>

```go
const (
    FieldVal   SelectionKind = iota // x.f is a struct field selector
    MethodVal                       // x.f is a method selector
    MethodExpr                      // x.f is a method expression
)
```

<a name="Signature"></a>

## type [Signature](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/signature.go#L21-L39>)

A Signature represents a \(non\-builtin\) function or method type. The receiver is ignored when comparing signatures for identity.

```go
type Signature struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewSignature"></a>

### func [NewSignature](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/signature.go#L49>)

```go
func NewSignature(recv *Var, params, results *Tuple, variadic bool) *Signature
```

NewSignature returns a new function type for the given receiver, parameters, and results, either of which may be nil. If variadic is set, the function is variadic, it must have at least one parameter, and the last parameter must be of unnamed slice type.

Deprecated: Use [NewSignatureType](<#NewSignatureType>) instead which allows for type parameters.

<a name="NewSignatureType"></a>

### func [NewSignatureType](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/signature.go#L68>)

```go
func NewSignatureType(recv *Var, recvTypeParams, typeParams []*TypeParam, params, results *Tuple, variadic bool) *Signature
```

NewSignatureType creates a new function type for the given receiver, receiver type parameters, type parameters, parameters, and results.

If variadic is set, params must hold at least one parameter and the last parameter must be an unnamed slice or a type parameter whose type set has an unnamed slice as common underlying type.

As a special case, to support append\(\[\]byte, str...\), for variadic signatures the last parameter may also be a string type, or a type parameter containing a mix of byte slices and string types in its type set. It may even be a named \[\]byte slice type resulting from instantiation of such a type parameter.

If recv is non\-nil, typeParams must be empty. If recvTypeParams is non\-empty, recv must be non\-nil.

<a name="Signature.Params"></a>

### func \(\*Signature\) [Params](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/signature.go#L138>)

```go
func (s *Signature) Params() *Tuple
```

Params returns the parameters of signature s, or nil. See [NewSignatureType](<#NewSignatureType>) for details of variadic functions.

<a name="Signature.Recv"></a>

### func \(\*Signature\) [Recv](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/signature.go#L128>)

```go
func (s *Signature) Recv() *Var
```

Recv returns the receiver of signature s \(if a method\), or nil if a function. It is ignored when comparing signatures for identity.

For an abstract method, Recv returns the enclosing interface either as a \*[Named](<#Named>) or an \*[Interface](<#Interface>). Due to embedding, an interface may contain methods whose receiver type is a different interface.

<a name="Signature.RecvTypeParams"></a>

### func \(\*Signature\) [RecvTypeParams](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/signature.go#L134>)

```go
func (s *Signature) RecvTypeParams() *TypeParamList
```

RecvTypeParams returns the receiver type parameters of signature s, or nil.

<a name="Signature.Results"></a>

### func \(\*Signature\) [Results](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/signature.go#L141>)

```go
func (s *Signature) Results() *Tuple
```

Results returns the results of signature s, or nil.

<a name="Signature.String"></a>

### func \(\*Signature\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/signature.go#L147>)

```go
func (s *Signature) String() string
```

<a name="Signature.TypeParams"></a>

### func \(\*Signature\) [TypeParams](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/signature.go#L131>)

```go
func (s *Signature) TypeParams() *TypeParamList
```

TypeParams returns the type parameters of signature s, or nil.

<a name="Signature.Underlying"></a>

### func \(\*Signature\) [Underlying](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/signature.go#L146>)

```go
func (s *Signature) Underlying() Type
```

<a name="Signature.Variadic"></a>

### func \(\*Signature\) [Variadic](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/signature.go#L144>)

```go
func (s *Signature) Variadic() bool
```

Variadic reports whether the signature s is variadic.

<a name="Sizes"></a>

## type [Sizes](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/sizes.go#L13-L28>)

Sizes defines the sizing functions for package unsafe.

```go
type Sizes interface {
    // Alignof returns the alignment of a variable of type T.
    // Alignof must implement the alignment guarantees required by the spec.
    // The result must be >= 1.
    Alignof(T Type) int64

    // Offsetsof returns the offsets of the given struct fields, in bytes.
    // Offsetsof must implement the offset guarantees required by the spec.
    // A negative entry in the result indicates that the struct is too large.
    Offsetsof(fields []*Var) []int64

    // Sizeof returns the size of a variable of type T.
    // Sizeof must implement the size guarantees required by the spec.
    // A negative result indicates that T is too large.
    Sizeof(T Type) int64
}
```

<a name="SizesFor"></a>

### func [SizesFor](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/sizes.go#L260>)

```go
func SizesFor(compiler, arch string) Sizes
```

SizesFor returns the Sizes used by a compiler for an architecture. The result is nil if a compiler/architecture pair is not known.

Supported architectures for compiler "gc": "386", "amd64", "amd64p32", "arm", "arm64", "loong64", "mips", "mipsle", "mips64", "mips64le", "ppc64", "ppc64le", "riscv64", "s390x", "sparc64", "wasm".

<a name="StdSizes"></a>

## type [StdSizes](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/sizes.go#L48-L51>)

StdSizes is a convenience type for creating commonly used Sizes. It makes the following simplifying assumptions:

- The size of explicitly sized basic types \(int16, etc.\) is the specified size.
- The size of strings and interfaces is 2\*WordSize.
- The size of slices is 3\*WordSize.
- The size of an array of n elements corresponds to the size of a struct of n consecutive fields of the array's element type.
- The size of a struct is the offset of the last field plus that field's size. As with all element types, if the struct is used in an array its size must first be aligned to a multiple of the struct's alignment.
- All other types have size WordSize.
- Arrays and structs are aligned per spec definition; all other types are naturally aligned with a maximum alignment MaxAlign.

\*StdSizes implements Sizes.

```go
type StdSizes struct {
    WordSize int64 // word size in bytes - must be >= 4 (32bits)
    MaxAlign int64 // maximum alignment in bytes - must be >= 1
}
```

<a name="StdSizes.Alignof"></a>

### func \(\*StdSizes\) [Alignof](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/sizes.go#L53>)

```go
func (s *StdSizes) Alignof(T Type) (result int64)
```

<a name="StdSizes.Offsetsof"></a>

### func \(\*StdSizes\) [Offsetsof](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/sizes.go#L129>)

```go
func (s *StdSizes) Offsetsof(fields []*Var) []int64
```

<a name="StdSizes.Sizeof"></a>

### func \(\*StdSizes\) [Sizeof](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/sizes.go#L167>)

```go
func (s *StdSizes) Sizeof(T Type) int64
```

<a name="Struct"></a>

## type [Struct](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/struct.go#L18-L21>)

A Struct represents a struct type.

```go
type Struct struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewStruct"></a>

### func [NewStruct](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/struct.go#L27>)

```go
func NewStruct(fields []*Var, tags []string) *Struct
```

NewStruct returns a new struct with the given fields and corresponding field tags. If a field with index i has a tag, tags\[i\] must be that tag, but len\(tags\) may be only as long as required to hold the tag with the largest index i. Consequently, if no field has a tag, tags may be nil.

<a name="Struct.Field"></a>

### func \(\*Struct\) [Field](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/struct.go#L46>)

```go
func (s *Struct) Field(i int) *Var
```

Field returns the i'th field for 0 \<= i \< NumFields\(\).

<a name="Struct.Fields"></a>

### func \(\*Struct\) [Fields](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/iter.go#L86>)

```go
func (s *Struct) Fields() iter.Seq[*Var]
```

Fields returns a go1.23 iterator over the fields of a struct type.

Example: for field := range s.Fields\(\) \{ ... \}

<a name="Struct.NumFields"></a>

### func \(\*Struct\) [NumFields](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/struct.go#L43>)

```go
func (s *Struct) NumFields() int
```

NumFields returns the number of fields in the struct \(including blank and embedded fields\).

<a name="Struct.String"></a>

### func \(\*Struct\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/struct.go#L57>)

```go
func (t *Struct) String() string
```

<a name="Struct.Tag"></a>

### func \(\*Struct\) [Tag](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/struct.go#L49>)

```go
func (s *Struct) Tag(i int) string
```

Tag returns the i'th field tag for 0 \<= i \< NumFields\(\).

<a name="Struct.Underlying"></a>

### func \(\*Struct\) [Underlying](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/struct.go#L56>)

```go
func (t *Struct) Underlying() Type
```

## type [Tuple](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/tuple.go#L13-L15>)

A Tuple represents an ordered list of variables; a nil \*Tuple is a valid \(empty\) tuple. Tuples are used as components of signatures and to represent the type of multiple assignments; they are not first class types of Go.

```go
type Tuple struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewTuple"></a>

### func [NewTuple](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/tuple.go#L18>)

```go
func NewTuple(x ...*Var) *Tuple
```

NewTuple returns a new tuple for the given variables.

<a name="Tuple.At"></a>

### func \(\*Tuple\) [At](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/tuple.go#L34>)

```go
func (t *Tuple) At(i int) *Var
```

At returns the i'th variable of tuple t.

<a name="Tuple.Len"></a>

### func \(\*Tuple\) [Len](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/tuple.go#L26>)

```go
func (t *Tuple) Len() int
```

Len returns the number variables of tuple t.

<a name="Tuple.String"></a>

```go

```



<a name="Type"></a>

## type [Type](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/type.go#L9-L18>)

A Type represents a type of Go. All types implement the Type interface.

```go
type Type interface {
    // Underlying returns the underlying type of a type.
    // Underlying types are never Named, TypeParam, or Alias types.
    //
    // See https://go.dev/ref/spec#Underlying_types.
    Underlying() Type

    // String returns a string representation of a type.
    String() string
}
```

<a name="Default"></a>

### func [Default](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/predicates.go#L523>)

```go
func Default(t Type) Type
```

Default returns the default "typed" type for an "untyped" type; it returns the incoming type for all other types. The default type for untyped nil is untyped nil.

<a name="Instantiate"></a>

### func [Instantiate](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/instantiate.go#L54>)

```go
func Instantiate(ctxt *Context, orig Type, targs []Type, validate bool) (Type, error)
```

Instantiate instantiates the type orig with the given type arguments targs. orig must be an \*Alias, \*Named, or \*Signature type. If there is no error, the resulting Type is an instantiated type of the same kind \(\*Alias, \*Named or \*Signature, respectively\).

Methods attached to a \*Named type are also instantiated, and associated with a new \*Func that has the same position as the original method, but nil function scope.

If ctxt is non\-nil, it may be used to de\-duplicate the instance against previous instances with the same identity. As a special case, generic \*Signature origin types are only considered identical if they are pointer equivalent, so that instantiating distinct \(but possibly identical\) signatures will yield different instances. The use of a shared context does not guarantee that identical instances are deduplicated in all cases.

If validate is set, Instantiate verifies that the number of type arguments and parameters match, and that the type arguments satisfy their respective type constraints. If verification fails, the resulting error may wrap an \*ArgumentError indicating which type argument did not satisfy its type parameter constraint, and why.

If validate is not set, Instantiate does not verify the type argument count or whether the type arguments satisfy their constraints. Instantiate is guaranteed to not return an error, but may panic. Specifically, for \*Signature types, Instantiate will panic immediately if the type argument count is incorrect; for \*Named types, a panic may occur later inside the \*Named API.

<a name="Unalias"></a>

### func [Unalias](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/alias.go#L104>)

```go
func Unalias(t Type) Type
```

Unalias returns t if it is not an alias type; otherwise it follows t's alias chain until it reaches a non\-alias type which is then returned. Consequently, the result is never an alias type. Returns nil if the alias is incomplete.

<a name="TypeAndValue"></a>

## type [TypeAndValue](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L391-L395>)

TypeAndValue reports the type and value \(for constants\) of the corresponding expression.

```go
type TypeAndValue struct {
    Type  Type
    Value constant.Value
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Eval"></a>

### func [Eval](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/eval.go#L24>)

```go
func Eval(fset *token.FileSet, pkg *Package, pos token.Pos, expr string) (_ TypeAndValue, err error)
```

Eval returns the type and, if constant, the value for the expression expr, evaluated at position pos of package pkg, which must have been derived from type\-checking an AST with complete position information relative to the provided file set.

The meaning of the parameters fset, pkg, and pos is the same as in [CheckExpr](<#CheckExpr>). An error is returned if expr cannot be parsed successfully, or the resulting expr AST cannot be type\-checked.

<a name="TypeAndValue.Addressable"></a>

### func \(TypeAndValue\) [Addressable](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L433>)

```go
func (tv TypeAndValue) Addressable() bool
```

Addressable reports whether the corresponding expression is addressable \(https://golang.org/ref/spec#Address_operators\).

<a name="TypeAndValue.Assignable"></a>

### func \(TypeAndValue\) [Assignable](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L439>)

```go
func (tv TypeAndValue) Assignable() bool
```

Assignable reports whether the corresponding expression is assignable to \(provided a value of the right type\).

<a name="TypeAndValue.HasOk"></a>

### func \(TypeAndValue\) [HasOk](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L445>)

```go
func (tv TypeAndValue) HasOk() bool
```

HasOk reports whether the corresponding expression may be used on the rhs of a comma\-ok assignment.

<a name="TypeAndValue.IsBuiltin"></a>

### func \(TypeAndValue\) [IsBuiltin](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L410>)

```go
func (tv TypeAndValue) IsBuiltin() bool
```

IsBuiltin reports whether the corresponding expression denotes a \(possibly parenthesized\) built\-in function.

<a name="TypeAndValue.IsNil"></a>

### func \(TypeAndValue\) [IsNil](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L427>)

```go
func (tv TypeAndValue) IsNil() bool
```

IsNil reports whether the corresponding expression denotes the predeclared value nil.

<a name="TypeAndValue.IsType"></a>

### func \(TypeAndValue\) [IsType](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L404>)

```go
func (tv TypeAndValue) IsType() bool
```

IsType reports whether the corresponding expression specifies a type.

<a name="TypeAndValue.IsValue"></a>

### func \(TypeAndValue\) [IsValue](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L417>)

```go
func (tv TypeAndValue) IsValue() bool
```

IsValue reports whether the corresponding expression is a value. Builtins are not considered values. Constant values have a non\- nil Value.

<a name="TypeAndValue.IsVoid"></a>

### func \(TypeAndValue\) [IsVoid](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/api.go#L399>)

```go
func (tv TypeAndValue) IsVoid() bool
```

IsVoid reports whether the corresponding expression is a function call without results.

<a name="TypeList"></a>

## type [TypeList](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/typelists.go#L31>)

TypeList holds a list of types.

```go
type TypeList struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="TypeList.At"></a>

### func \(\*TypeList\) [At](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/typelists.go#L46>)

```go
func (l *TypeList) At(i int) Type
```

At returns the i'th type in the list.

<a name="TypeList.Len"></a>

### func \(\*TypeList\) [Len](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/typelists.go#L43>)

```go
func (l *TypeList) Len() int
```

Len returns the number of types in the list. It is safe to call on a nil receiver.

<a name="TypeList.Types"></a>

### func \(\*TypeList\) [Types](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/iter.go#L151>)

```go
func (l *TypeList) Types() iter.Seq[Type]
```

Types returns a go1.23 iterator over the elements of a list of types.

Example: for t := range l.Types\(\) \{ ... \}

<a name="TypeName"></a>

## type [TypeName](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L242-L244>)

A TypeName is an [Object](<#Object>) that represents a type with a name: a defined type \([Named](<#Named>)\), an alias type \([Alias](<#Alias>)\), a type parameter \([TypeParam](<#TypeParam>)\), or a predeclared type such as int or error.

```go
type TypeName struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewTypeName"></a>

### func [NewTypeName](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L253>)

```go
func NewTypeName(pos token.Pos, pkg *Package, name string, typ Type) *TypeName
```

NewTypeName returns a new type name denoting the given typ. The remaining arguments set the attributes found with all Objects.

The typ argument may be a defined \(Named\) type or an alias type. It may also be nil such that the returned TypeName can be used as argument for NewNamed, which will set the TypeName's type as a side\- effect.

<a name="TypeName.IsAlias"></a>

### func \(\*TypeName\) [IsAlias](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L267>)

```go
func (obj *TypeName) IsAlias() bool
```

IsAlias reports whether obj is an alias name for a type.

<a name="TypeName.String"></a>

### func \(\*TypeName\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L648>)

```go
func (obj *TypeName) String() string
```

<a name="TypeParam"></a>

## type [TypeParam](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/typeparam.go#L25-L31>)

A TypeParam represents the type of a type parameter in a generic declaration.

A TypeParam has a name; use the [TypeParam.Obj](<#TypeParam.Obj>) method to access its [TypeName](<#TypeName>) object.

```go
type TypeParam struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewTypeParam"></a>

### func [NewTypeParam](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/typeparam.go#L39>)

```go
func NewTypeParam(obj *TypeName, constraint Type) *TypeParam
```

NewTypeParam returns a new TypeParam. Type parameters may be set on a Named type by calling SetTypeParams. Setting a type parameter on more than one type will result in a panic.

The constraint argument can be nil, and set later via SetConstraint. If the constraint is non\-nil, it must be fully defined.

<a name="TypeParam.Constraint"></a>

### func \(\*TypeParam\) [Constraint](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/typeparam.go#L75>)

```go
func (t *TypeParam) Constraint() Type
```

Constraint returns the type constraint specified for t.

<a name="TypeParam.Index"></a>

### func \(\*TypeParam\) [Index](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/typeparam.go#L70>)

```go
func (t *TypeParam) Index() int
```

Index returns the index of the type param within its param list, or \-1 if the type parameter has not yet been bound to a type.

<a name="TypeParam.Obj"></a>

### func \(\*TypeParam\) [Obj](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/typeparam.go#L66>)

```go
func (t *TypeParam) Obj() *TypeName
```

Obj returns the type name for the type parameter t.

<a name="TypeParam.SetConstraint"></a>

### func \(\*TypeParam\) [SetConstraint](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/typeparam.go#L85>)

```go
func (t *TypeParam) SetConstraint(bound Type)
```

SetConstraint sets the type constraint for t.

It must be called by users of NewTypeParam after the bound's underlying is fully defined, and before using the type parameter in any way other than to form other types. Once SetConstraint returns the receiver, t is safe for concurrent use.

<a name="TypeParam.String"></a>

### func \(\*TypeParam\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/typeparam.go#L103>)

```go
func (t *TypeParam) String() string
```

<a name="TypeParam.Underlying"></a>

### func \(\*TypeParam\) [Underlying](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/typeparam.go#L99>)

```go
func (t *TypeParam) Underlying() Type
```

Underlying returns the [underlying type](<https://go.dev/ref/spec#Underlying_types.>) of the type parameter t, which is the underlying type of its constraint. This type is always an interface.

<a name="TypeParamList"></a>

## type [TypeParamList](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/typelists.go#L11>)

TypeParamList holds a list of type parameters.

```go
type TypeParamList struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

```go
func (l *TypeParamList) At(i int) *TypeParam
```

At returns the i'th type parameter in the list.

### func \(\*TypeParamList\) [Len](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/typelists.go#L15>)

```go
func (l *TypeParamList) Len() int
```

Len returns the number of type parameters in the list. It is safe to call on a nil receiver.

<a name="TypeParamList.TypeParams"></a>

### func \(\*TypeParamList\) [TypeParams](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/iter.go#L138>)


TypeParams returns a go1.23 iterator over a list of type parameters.

Example: for tparam := range l.TypeParams\(\) \{ ... \}

<a name="Union"></a>

## type [Union](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/union.go#L17-L19>)

A Union represents a union of terms embedded in an interface.

```go
type Union struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewUnion"></a>

### func [NewUnion](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/union.go#L23>)

```go
func NewUnion(terms []*Term) *Union
```

NewUnion returns a new [Union](<#Union>) type with the given terms. It is an error to create an empty union; they are syntactically not possible.

<a name="Union.Len"></a>

### func \(\*Union\) [Len](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/union.go#L30>)

```go
func (u *Union) Len() int
```

```go
func (u *Union) String() string
```

```go
func (u *Union) Term(i int) *Term
```

<a name="Union.Terms"></a>

### func \(\*Union\) [Terms](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/iter.go#L125>)

```go
func (u *Union) Terms() iter.Seq[*Term]
```

Terms returns a go1.23 iterator over the terms of a union.

Example: for term := range union.Terms\(\) \{ ... \}

### func \(\*Union\) [Underlying](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/union.go#L33>)

```go
func (u *Union) Underlying() Type
```

<a name="Var"></a>

## type [Var](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L295-L300>)

A Var represents a declared variable \(including function parameters and results, and struct fields\).

```go
type Var struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewField"></a>

### func [NewField](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L359>)

```go
func NewField(pos token.Pos, pkg *Package, name string, typ Type, embedded bool) *Var
```

NewField returns a new variable representing a struct field. For embedded fields, the name is the unqualified type name under which the field is accessible.

<a name="NewParam"></a>

### func [NewParam](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L352>)

```go
func NewParam(pos token.Pos, pkg *Package, name string, typ Type) *Var
```

NewParam returns a new variable representing a function parameter.

The caller must subsequently call [Var.SetKind](<#Var.SetKind>) if the desired Var is not of kind [ParamVar](<#PackageVar>): for example, [RecvVar](<#PackageVar>) or [ResultVar](<#PackageVar>).

<a name="NewVar"></a>

### func [NewVar](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L344>)

```go
func NewVar(pos token.Pos, pkg *Package, name string, typ Type) *Var
```

NewVar returns a new variable. The arguments set the attributes found with all Objects.

The caller must subsequently call [Var.SetKind](<#Var.SetKind>) if the desired Var is not of kind [PackageVar](<#PackageVar>).

<a name="Var.Anonymous"></a>

### func \(\*Var\) [Anonymous](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L373>)

```go
func (obj *Var) Anonymous() bool
```

Anonymous reports whether the variable is an embedded field. Same as Embedded; only present for backward\-compatibility.

<a name="Var.Embedded"></a>

### func \(\*Var\) [Embedded](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L376>)

```go
func (obj *Var) Embedded() bool
```

Embedded reports whether the variable is an embedded field.

<a name="Var.IsField"></a>

### func \(\*Var\) [IsField](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L379>)

```go
func (obj *Var) IsField() bool
```

IsField reports whether the variable is a struct field.

<a name="Var.Kind"></a>

### func \(\*Var\) [Kind](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L333>)

```go
func (v *Var) Kind() VarKind
```

Kind reports what kind of variable v is.

<a name="Var.Origin"></a>

### func \(\*Var\) [Origin](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L388>)

```go
func (obj *Var) Origin() *Var
```

Origin returns the canonical Var for its receiver, i.e. the Var object recorded in Info.Defs.

For synthetic Vars created during instantiation \(such as struct fields or function parameters that depend on type arguments\), this will be the corresponding Var on the generic \(uninstantiated\) type. For all other Vars Origin returns the receiver.

<a name="Var.SetKind"></a>

### func \(\*Var\) [SetKind](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L337>)

```go
func (v *Var) SetKind(kind VarKind)
```

SetKind sets the kind of the variable. It should be used only immediately after [NewVar](<#NewVar>) or [NewParam](<#NewParam>).

<a name="Var.String"></a>

### func \(\*Var\) [String](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L649>)

```go
func (obj *Var) String() string
```

<a name="VarKind"></a>

## type [VarKind](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/go/types/object.go#L303>)

A VarKind discriminates the various kinds of variables.

```go
type VarKind uint8
```

```go
func (kind VarKind) String() string
```
